name: Roblón de El Monasterio Trail
name_original: Senda del Roblón del Monasterio (PRC-P 12) — circular trailrunning desde Velilla del Río Carrión
description: Jog the difficult 10.0-mile Roblón de El Monasterio Trail through diverse forests to a magnificent ancient oak and monastery ruins.
region_intro: Jogging around Velilla del Río Carrión offers diverse terrain within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park. The region features a mountainous landscape with prominent peaks and varied inclines, alongside picturesque riverine paths along the Carrión River and routes tracing the Compuerto and Camporredondo reservoirs. Significant forested areas, including relict pine forests, provide shaded trails and unique environments for running. Best jogging routes around Velilla del Río Carrión The most popular jogging route is Espigüete East Ridge and Mazobre Waterfall Loop, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 23 minutes to complete, offering views of the emblematic Espigüete peak. Another top favourite among local runners is Peña Prieta y Peña del infierno por collado del Ves — circular trailrunning por el Parque Natural Montaña Palentina, a difficult 11.8 miles (19.0 km) path through the Montaña Palentina Natural Park. Local runners also love the Roblón de El Monasterio Trail, a 10.0 miles (16.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation changes, often completed in about 2 hours 3 minutes. Jogging around Velilla del Río Carrión is defined by impressive mountains, serene river valleys, expansive reservoirs, and unique pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging mountain trails to more gentle riverside paths. The routes in Velilla del Río Carrión are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Velilla del Río Carrión's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Velilla del Río Carrión?
A: There are over 18 running routes around Velilla del Río Carrión, offering a wide range of options for different abilities and preferences within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in this region?
A: The region offers a diverse mix of terrain. You'll find mountainous landscapes with varied inclines, picturesque riverside paths along the Carrión River, routes tracing the Compuerto and Camporredondo reservoirs, and significant forested areas, including unique relict pine forests, providing shaded trails.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Velilla del Río Carrión?
A: While many routes are rated as difficult, the region does offer more gentle options. The &lt;em&gt;Senda de los Pescadores&lt;/em&gt;, for instance, is described as a flat route following the Carrión River, ideal for a gentle jog. Look for routes along the river valleys or reservoir edges for less challenging terrain.

Q: What are some challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Velilla del Río Carrión offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1118901197/pena-prieta-y-pena-del-infierno-por-collado-del-ves-circular-trailrunning-por-el-parque-natural-montana-palentina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Prieta y Peña del infierno por collado del Ves&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 19.0 km (11.8 miles) trail with significant elevation changes through the Montaña Palentina Natural Park. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38145722" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado del Ves – Lago de las Lomas loop from Parking Pinollano&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 33 km with substantial ascent.

Q: Are there any scenic running routes that pass by natural landmarks or points of interest?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer stunning scenery. The &lt;em&gt;Ruta de los Pantanos&lt;/em&gt; provides expansive views of the Compuerto and Camporredondo reservoirs. You can also find trails that pass by cultural points like the Trout Interpretation Center, the Fuente de la Reana, and historical ermitas. For impressive mountain views, consider routes that offer glimpses of peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3487262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Espigüete Summit&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5113975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Prieta Peak&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Velilla del Río Carrión?
A: The running trails in Velilla del Río Carrión are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the impressive mountains, serene river valleys, expansive reservoirs, and unique pine forests that define the area's diverse and beautiful landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, there are circular running routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1118928927/espigueete-east-ridge-and-mazobre-waterfall-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Espigüete East Ridge and Mazobre Waterfall Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 9.5 km (5.9 miles) trail that allows you to return to your starting point while enjoying views of the emblematic Espigüete peak and Mazobre Waterfall.

Q: Can I find long-distance running trails in the area?
A: Yes, Velilla del Río Carrión is excellent for long-distance running. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31449033" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lago de las Lomas loop from Cardaño de Arriba&lt;/a&gt; extend for over 20 km (12.4 miles), offering endurance challenges through varied mountain terrain.

Q: Are there any specific forested trails for running?
A: The &lt;em&gt;Senda del Pinar de Velilla&lt;/em&gt;, starting near the Colonia del Brezo, offers a tranquil and scenic jogging experience through an ancient pine forest. This area is home to one of the two relict pine forests in the Cantabrian Mountains, providing a unique and shaded environment.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Velilla del Río Carrión?
A: The diverse terrain means you can find suitable routes year-round. During warmer months, shaded forest paths like the &lt;em&gt;Senda del Pinar de Velilla&lt;/em&gt; or riverside trails are ideal. In cooler seasons, the lower elevation paths along the Carrión River or reservoirs can be very pleasant. Always check local weather conditions, especially for higher mountain routes.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by waterfalls?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1118928927/espigueete-east-ridge-and-mazobre-waterfall-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Espigüete East Ridge and Mazobre Waterfall Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights the Mazobre Waterfall as a feature along its path, offering a refreshing sight during your run.

Q: Are there any huts or shelters along the running routes?
A: Yes, within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, you can find several shelters. For example, there's a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5837344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Free shelter in Parque Natural Montaña Palentina&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5144390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albergue de Villaverde&lt;/a&gt;, which can be useful for longer runs or in case of unexpected weather changes.
tips:
Beautiful town in the Palencia Mountain located, as its name indicates, on the banks of the Carrión River. It is a good enclave to go out and discover this Natural Park.
During the last century its main industry was coal mining, although now it has closed, which has caused a sharp decline in the population.

Beautiful and emblematic tree that owes its name to the old Benedictine monastery located a few meters from it. To get to this point, you have to follow a path that runs through a beautiful beech forest dotted with magnificent oaks.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Roblón de El Monasterio Trail?
faqs.answer: The starting point for many routes, including this one, is often near Velilla del Río Carrión. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, routes commonly begin near a shooting range, which usually offers parking facilities. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is the Roblón de El Monasterio Trail suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: This specific route is rated as difficult for jogging, covering over 16 km with more than 430 meters of elevation gain. It requires very good physical fitness due to varied terrain and significant ascents. While some shorter walking routes in the area might be easier, this trail is not recommended for beginner joggers.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a diverse and engaging experience. You'll encounter a mix of paved surfaces, accessible paths, and unpaved sections. It traverses through various forest types, including juniper, oak, beech, and holm oak groves. Be prepared for forest tracks that can be rocky and stony in places, alongside sections with softer soil. There are both relatively flat parts and significant elevation changes.

faqs.question: What notable features and landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The most significant feature is the magnificent Roblón de El Monasterio, a centenary pedunculate oak. You'll also pass the Stone Bridge of Velilla del Río Carrión. Near the Roblón, you can find scattered remains of an ancient Benedictine monastery. The route also offers panoramic views of Velilla del Río Carrión and glimpses into the region's mining history with old structures like the lavaderos de Besande.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed across seasons, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures and vibrant foliage. Summer can be warm, while winter jogging is possible but requires preparation for potentially cold and snowy conditions, especially at higher elevations. Always check local weather forecasts before you go.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Roblón de El Monasterio Trail?
faqs.answer: As the trail is located within the Parque Natural de Fuentes Carrionas y Fuente Cobre, dogs are generally permitted but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always practice responsible pet ownership and pack out any waste.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this trail or the natural park?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no specific permits required or entrance fees to jog the Roblón de El Monasterio Trail or to access the Parque Natural de Fuentes Carrionas y Fuente Cobre. However, it's always good practice to respect park regulations and leave no trace.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Senda Roblón de El Monasterio, Ruta de las Cuencas Mineras del Carbón, Camino del Pando, Sendero a Torales, Camino Hondo, and Puente de la Maderada. This offers a varied experience through the local trail network.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers rich and varied scenery. You'll enjoy beautiful forested areas, including unique juniper forests on the slopes of Peña Lampa and impressive beech forests in Valdehaya. From certain points, particularly near the ancient monastery site, you can take in panoramic views of Velilla del Río Carrión and surrounding villages.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Roblón de El Monasterio Trail by jogging?
faqs.answer: For an average jogger, this 16 km route, with its significant elevation changes and difficult rating, typically takes around 2 hours and 3 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or pubs near the trailhead in Velilla del Río Carrión?
faqs.answer: Velilla del Río Carrión, being the nearest town, offers various amenities. You can expect to find cafes, pubs, and potentially some accommodation options there to refuel or relax before or after your jog. It's a good idea to check local listings for current operating hours.

waypoints.name: Stone Bridge of Velilla del Río Carrión
